element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Base combat tool player must master from start
Allows latching onto hooks and climbing; expands vertical traversal

element_introduction_order.first_appears: First stage of subsequent element plane
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Breaks through certain walls; opens previously inaccessible paths
Combinable for temporary buffs: elemental sword enhancement, jump height increase

element_introduction_order.first_appears: After completing terrestrial plane (4 element planes)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Considerably harder difficulty spike; boss encounters; final progression gate

typical_tension_triggers: Hourglass timer pressure (time limit per plane)
Boss encounters at end of each level
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: High tension sustained throughout with timer pressure; difficulty escalates significantly in second half; non-linear plane selection allows tension modulation but creates circular repetition stress

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: New weapons unlock access to previously blocked areas; wall-breaking hammer and grappling hook act as spatial keys

difficulty_curve.early_game: Moderate; introduces basic platforming and twin sword combat; single weapon per plane learned in first stage
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating; accumulating weapon mechanics (grappling hook, hammer) increase spatial complexity; potion combination system adds resource management
difficulty_curve.late_game: Severe spike; elemental planes of evil explicitly noted as 'considerably harder than initial four sets'; final boss after credits with indefinite cosmic evil loop
notable_spikes: Transition to elemental planes of evil (explicit difficulty jump)
Final boss encounter post-credits (designed to withhold satisfaction/ending)
Hourglass time pressure mechanic forces plane restart if exceeded

notable_level_patterns.description: Central hub stage with non-linear access to four element planes (earth, air, fire, water), each containing three levels. Player can choose order of engagement but must complete all planes to progress. Non-linear structure creates circular repetition if death occurs.

New weapon acquired in first stage of each element plane; unlocks previously inaccessible areas. Grappling hook enables climbing; hammer breaks walls. Encourages backtracking or plane-switching strategy.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: First stage of each element plane

notable_level_patterns.description: Hourglass timer at screen bottom measures time to complete all three levels of current plane. Timeout returns player to hub and forces plane restart. Creates persistent tension and consequence for slow play.
Enemy boss encounter at end of each of three levels per plane. Defeating all bosses in a plane opens access to next elemental progression.

notable_level_patterns.pattern_name: Difficulty Cliff - Elemental Planes of Evil
notable_level_patterns.description: Second cycle of four planes (fire, earth, wind, water evil versions) features explicitly harder level design and boss encounters. Designed to reinforce narrative theme of endless struggle.
